global_growth_2026_why: EdRAM still sees global growth holding up rather than collapsing.
global_growth_2026_raw_wording: bonne tenue de la croissance mondiale
us_gdp_2026_why: EdRAM describes US growth as soft and uncertain, not as a clean point forecast.
us_gdp_2026_raw_wording: la croissance est atone et incertaine aux Etats-Unis
eurozone_gdp_2026_why: Europe should benefit in 2026 from German fiscal spending and being earlier in the cycle.
eurozone_gdp_2026_raw_wording: l'Europe ... devrait beneficier des depenses budgetaires allemandes en 2026
eurozone_inflation_2026_why: EdRAM says eurozone inflation has now reached the ECB's 2% objective.
fed_year_end_rate_2026_why: Fed policy could become more accommodative after Powell's departure.
fed_year_end_rate_2026_raw_wording: il parait raisonnable d'envisager une banque centrale plus accommodante
ecb_deposit_rate_year_end_2026_why: ECB may have to ease if a more dovish Fed drives euro appreciation and hurts competitiveness.
ecb_deposit_rate_year_end_2026_raw_wordi: la BCE pourrait etre contrainte d'assouplir sa politique si la Fed engage une nouvelle baisse de taux
eur_usd_2026_why: EdRAM discusses the euro around 1.16-1.19 and highlights appreciation risk if the Fed eases again.
eur_usd_2026_raw_wording: Il a finalement atteint 1,19 en seance, mais evolue depuis le mois de juillet autour de 1,16.
equities_overall_why: EdRAM starts 2026 with a relatively balanced but cautious allocation between equities and bonds.
equities_overall_raw_wording: une allocation relativement equilibree entre actions et obligations, tout en conservant un biais prudent
equities_geography_europe_why: Europe is one of the main positive equity areas thanks to Germany, NextGen EU, and domestic recovery.
equities_geography_china_why: EdRAM explicitly says China is difficult to ignore in allocation because its AI model is cheaper and more pragmatic.
equities_geography_china_raw_wording: il apparait difficile d'ignorer totalement ce marche dans une allocation
equities_size_large_cap_why: EdRAM remains very underweight the US AI mega-caps.
equities_size_large_cap_raw_wording: nous restons tres sous-ponderes sur les Magnificent 7 (MAG7)
equities_size_small_cap_why: European small caps are one of the clearest preferred benchmark expressions.
equities_style_value_why: US value is explicitly part of the diversification basket.
equities_style_value_raw_wording: Les valeurs americaines Value ont egalement leur place
fixed_income_overall_why: EdRAM is constructive on credit carry even while staying cautious on long sovereign duration.
fixed_income_overall_raw_wording: L'annee 2026 s'annonce favorable aux marches de credit
fixed_income_credit_quality_investment_g: positive
Investment-grade issuers remain a preferred universe, especially for hybrids.
dans un univers d'emetteurs Investment Grade
fixed_income_credit_quality_high_yield_w: EdRAM explicitly says high yield looks particularly attractive for 2026.
fixed_income_credit_quality_high_yield_r: le segment High Yield se revele particulierement attractif
fixed_income_duration_why: EdRAM prefers credit risk to duration risk and does not favor long-maturity bonds.
fixed_income_duration_raw_wording: nous ne privilegions pas les obligations a maturite longue ... nous privilegions le risque de credit au risque de duration
fixed_income_segment_government_bonds_wh: EdRAM does not favor long-dated sovereign bonds because term premium rebuilding is incomplete.
fixed_income_segment_government_bonds_ra: nous ne privilegions pas les obligations a maturite longue
fixed_income_segment_corporate_bonds_why: Financial and corporate hybrids are explicitly preferred.
fixed_income_segment_corporate_bonds_raw: les dettes hybrides financieres et d'entreprises
fixed_income_segment_em_debt_why: Emerging debt should benefit from Fed easing.
fixed_income_segment_em_debt_raw_wording: la dette emergente, qui devrait profiter de l'assouplissement monetaire de la Fed
currencies_usd_why: The benchmark discussion centers on dollar weakness and a more accommodative Fed.
currencies_usd_raw_wording: si la Fed engage une nouvelle baisse de taux
currencies_eur_why: EdRAM highlights euro appreciation risk versus the dollar.
currencies_eur_raw_wording: afin de contenir l'appreciation de l'euro
commodities_gold_why: Gold miners could benefit if the Fed becomes more politicized and accommodative.
commodities_gold_raw_wording: Les valeurs auriferes ... pourraient a nouveau tirer leur epingle du jeu
themes_ai_why: EdRAM treats AI as a speculative bubble in US mega-caps but remains constructive on Big Data users and Chinese AI pragmatism.
themes_ai_raw_wording: nous restons tres sous-ponderes sur les Magnificent 7 ... privilegions plutot le theme du Big Data
themes_healthcare_why: Healthcare is explicitly presented as an under-owned recovery theme.
themes_healthcare_raw_wording: Le secteur de la sante repond egalement a ces caracteristiques
themes_geopolitical_risk_why: The outlook repeatedly cites US tariffs, Chinese dumping, and political pressure on the Fed as active risks.
themes_geopolitical_risk_raw_wording: entre dumping chinois et tarifs americains
themes_defense_sovereignty_why: Resilience and sovereignty are explicit benchmark themes, especially in Europe.
themes_defense_sovereignty_raw_wording: les themes de la resilience et de la souverainete
themes_other_why: Big Data and resilience are explicit named benchmark themes beyond the standard buckets.

fixed_income_segment_government_bonds_wh: Anima is moderately constructive on developed-market sovereign bonds because elevated yields should still support returns in 2026.
fixed_income_segment_government_bonds_ra: Siamo moderatamente costruttivi sui mercati obbligazionari dei Paesi sviluppati nel 2026. Ci aspettiamo che le performance dei titoli governativi siano sostenute dai livelli storicamente elevati dei rendimenti.

equities_geography_us_why: Amundi keeps an equal-weight US stance: Fed cuts are supportive, but concentration and valuation risks limit upside.
equities_geography_us_raw_wording: Globalement neutre, renforcement de cette equiponderation pour aller au-dela du risque de concentration
equities_geography_japan_why: Japan remains supported medium term, but Amundi shifts to neutral in H1 2026 before potentially adding later in the year.
equities_geography_japan_raw_wording: Passage a neutre au S1, avant de renforcer au S2 lors des politiques pro-croissance
fixed_income_overall_why: Amundi stays constructive on fixed income through euro credit and emerging debt, while remaining selective on duration and spread risk.
fixed_income_overall_raw_wording: La dette emergente en devise forte reste attractive
fixed_income_credit_quality_high_yield_w: Amundi is selective on high yield: US and European HY look vulnerable, but selective higher-carry EM exposure still has value.
fixed_income_credit_quality_high_yield_r: La categorie est plus vulnerable aux signes de faiblesse des entreprises
currencies_eur_why: Amundi's EUR/USD forecast and weak-dollar stance imply euro appreciation versus the dollar into late 2026.

equities_geography_japan_why: UBS stays constructive on Japan thanks to supportive policy, earnings recovery, better returns on equity, and lower valuations.
equities_geography_japan_raw_wording: Japan. While political uncertainty may contribute to volatility in the near term, we expect more accommodative policies under the new government to support the market over the medium term.
fixed_income_credit_quality_high_yield_w: UBS is cautious on high yield because spreads are tight, but still sees a role for select high yield inside diversified fixed income strategies.
fixed_income_credit_quality_high_yield_r: We are more cautious on riskier parts of fixed income like high yield given very tight spreads. Nonetheless, we see merit in diversified fixed income strategies.
commodities_overall_why: UBS says commodities are attractive for 2026 as supply constraints and rising demand support the complex.
commodities_overall_raw_wording: Commodities look attractive for 2026. Supply constraints and rising demand support energy, metals, and agriculture; precious metals remain effective diversifiers.
commodities_industrial_metals_why: Industrial metals, especially copper and aluminum, should benefit from tight supply and AI and energy-transition demand.
commodities_industrial_metals_raw_wordin: Looking ahead to 2026, both copper and aluminum should continue to benefit from improving fundamentals.
currencies_overall_why: UBS frames FX as an active source of return and prefers long positions in the euro and other higher-yielding currencies against the dollar.
currencies_overall_raw_wording: We favor long positions in the euro, Australian dollar, and Norwegian krone versus the USD. Our preference is for high-yielding currencies.

global_growth_2026_why: Lombard Odier expects world growth to stay moderate in 2026 rather than giving a clean point estimate in the text.
global_growth_2026_raw_wording: Nous tablons sur une croissance mondiale a nouveau moderee en 2026.
us_gdp_2026_why: The US is expected to grow below trend and below potential in 2026, but the narrative is directional rather than a precise point forecast.
us_gdp_2026_raw_wording: Nous anticipons un nouvel affaiblissement du marche de l'emploi et une croissance inferieure a son potentiel.
eurozone_gdp_2026_why: Eurozone growth should be modest and slightly above 1%, supported by Germany and EU spending.
eurozone_gdp_2026_raw_wording: Nous tablons sur une croissance moderee depassant legerement 1% dans la zone euro.
china_gdp_2026_why: China should slow only gradually to a little above 4% in 2026; the source wording is approximate rather than a strict table forecast.
china_gdp_2026_raw_wording: la croissance chinoise ... devrait passer a un peu plus de 4% en 2026.
us_inflation_2026_why: US inflation is expected to remain above target in 2026, peaking just above 3% early in the year.
us_inflation_2026_raw_wording: nous pensons que l'indice des depenses de consommation personnelle terminera l'annee 2026 au-dessus de l'objectif, apres avoir atteint un pic d'un peu plus de 3% en debut d'annee
eurozone_inflation_2026_why: Eurozone inflation should stay broadly aligned with the ECB target through 2026.
eurozone_inflation_2026_raw_wording: avec des taux d'interet inchanges et une inflation globalement alignee sur l'objectif tout au long de l'annee
equities_geography_europe_why: Lombard Odier sees Europe benefiting from fiscal easing, better earnings support, and defense and infrastructure spending.
equities_geography_europe_raw_wording: En Europe, les valorisations sont superieures aux moyennes historiques, mais la croissance des benefices devrait profiter de l'assouplissement budgetaire
equities_geography_china_why: Chinese technology is treated as an attractive equity opportunity because earnings growth is strong, valuations are better, and AI adoption is faster.
equities_geography_china_raw_wording: le secteur technologique chinois offre une croissance des benefices similaire, de meilleures valorisations, une adoption plus rapide de l'IA
equities_style_quality_why: The preferred equity style is quality growth at a reasonable price, alongside dividend payers.
equities_style_quality_raw_wording: nous privilegions les actions conjuguant croissance de qualite et prix raisonnable
fixed_income_credit_quality_investment_g: mixed
Corporate bond spreads are tight and likely to limit upside, so Lombard Odier is selective rather than broadly constructive on developed-market credit.
L'ecart de rendement des obligations d'entreprise s'est encore resserre en 2025.
fixed_income_credit_quality_high_yield_w: Tight spreads make high-yield corporate bonds less attractive in 2026.
fixed_income_credit_quality_high_yield_r: les obligations d'entreprise de qualite et a haut rendement ne sont pas pour autant attractives
fixed_income_segment_government_bonds_wh: Developed-market sovereign bonds should underperform emerging debt and offer limited capital gains.
fixed_income_segment_government_bonds_ra: Les obligations souveraines des marches developpes devraient sous-performer leurs consoeurs emergentes.
currencies_eur_why: Lombard Odier expects euro appreciation versus the dollar toward 1.22.
currencies_eur_raw_wording: Nous tablons sur une appreciation de l'euro aux alentours de 1,22 contre dollar.
themes_geopolitical_risk_why: Geopolitical tensions are explicitly treated as downside risks that can hit energy markets and supply chains.
themes_geopolitical_risk_raw_wording: les tensions geopolitiques qui pourraient se repercuter sur les marches de l'energie et perturber les chaines d'approvisionnement

equities_geography_china_why: Carmignac sees distinct AI-related opportunities in China alongside other non-US technology exposures.
equities_geography_china_raw_wording: la Chine developpe son propre ecosysteme IA, ouvrant la voie a des opportunites distinctes
equities_style_growth_why: The equity stance explicitly includes technology leaders and software beneficiaries of AI adoption.
equities_style_growth_raw_wording: Nous associons les leaders technologiques aux valeurs defensives
fixed_income_segment_corporate_bonds_why: Carmignac prefers public credit from solid issuers and highlights investment-grade defense corporates as an attractive way to gain exposure.
fixed_income_segment_corporate_bonds_raw: les marches de credit publics sont par construction plus conservateurs
themes_geopolitical_risk_why: Persistent tariffs and geopolitical tensions are explicit headwinds in Carmignac's 2026 framework.
themes_geopolitical_risk_raw_wording: persistance des tensions geopolitiques (tarifs, Russie, Taiwan, Venezuela)

global_growth_2026_why: Pictet expects the policy backdrop to support global growth in 2026, but the text gives a directional view rather than a clean point forecast.
global_growth_2026_raw_wording: Le contexte favorable devrait soutenir la croissance mondiale en 2026
us_gdp_2026_why: US growth should accelerate early in the year on budget support and trade stabilization, but no explicit numeric forecast is given in the text.
us_gdp_2026_raw_wording: Aux Etats-Unis, la croissance devrait s'accelerer en debut d'annee
eurozone_gdp_2026_why: Europe's recovery is tied to German fiscal expansion and broader integration, but not disclosed as a precise 2026 GDP number in the narrative.
eurozone_gdp_2026_raw_wording: En Europe, la reprise s'appuiera sur le vaste programme budgetaire de l'Allemagne.
china_gdp_2026_why: China should improve later as trade uncertainty fades and stimulus is deployed, but the text does not provide a clean point estimate.
china_gdp_2026_raw_wording: Une acceleration plus tardive est attendue en Chine.
us_inflation_2026_why: Pictet expects US inflation to tick back up in 2026 without giving a precise printed level.
us_inflation_2026_raw_wording: L'inflation devrait remonter d'un cran aux Etats-Unis
eurozone_inflation_2026_why: Eurozone inflation is expected to slow in 2026, but the source remains directional.
eurozone_inflation_2026_raw_wording: ralentir au sein de la zone euro
fed_year_end_rate_2026_why: Pictet expects the Fed to keep cutting through 2026 under a more growth-supportive leadership stance.
fed_year_end_rate_2026_raw_wording: La Fed devrait poursuivre ses baisses de taux en 2026
equities_geography_em_why: Pictet explicitly links its China margin-recovery thesis to a broader positive stance on emerging markets.
equities_geography_em_raw_wording: d'ou notre optimisme a l'egard des marches emergents

fixed_income_duration_why: Robeco prefers shorter spread duration because tight investment-grade spreads and higher long-end yields limit upside.
fixed_income_duration_raw_wording: Our view of long-end yields ending 2026 higher, reinforces a preference for shorter spread duration exposure.
commodities_industrial_metals_why: Industrial metals should rally if the synchronized cyclical upswing lifts manufacturing activity.
commodities_industrial_metals_raw_wordin: we think that industrial metals have room to rally
commodities_overall_why: Robeco likes gold and industrial metals, but remains neutral on oil, so the broader commodity stance is positive but differentiated.
commodities_overall_raw_wording: industrial metals have room to rally ... Commodities Gold + Oil =

equities_geography_japan_why: Generali is neutral on Japan in the short term but remains overweight on a medium-term horizon thanks to fiscal stimulus, banks, tech momentum, and buybacks.
equities_geography_japan_raw_wording: Japan is neutral short term but still OW on a mid-term horizon
themes_geopolitical_risk_why: Trade-war escalation and broader fragmentation remain explicit downside risks in the 2026 house view.
themes_geopolitical_risk_raw_wording: a renewed escalation of the trade war with the US remains a key area to watch

themes_defense_sovereignty_why: European military and infrastructure spending is presented as a key support for regional recovery and investment opportunities.
themes_defense_sovereignty_raw_wording: Germany plans to increase spending on their military and infrastructure, and other countries are following suit.

equities_geography_europe_why: PGIM sees European equities as attractively valued even though macro and tariff headwinds remain.
equities_geography_europe_raw_wording: European equities offer more attractive valuations compared to the U.S.
equities_geography_japan_why: Japanese equities are supported by double-digit earnings expectations, fiscal expansion, and governance reforms.
equities_geography_japan_raw_wording: Japanese earnings expectations point to double-digit growth
equities_geography_em_why: Emerging-market assets should benefit from resilient global growth and trade dynamics, with strong earnings growth expectations.
equities_geography_em_raw_wording: Emerging market assets are positioned to benefit from resilient global growth and trade dynamics
currencies_em_fx_why: PGIM explicitly expects EMFX to benefit from a weakening U.S. dollar.
currencies_em_fx_raw_wording: in EMFX, we expect the U.S. dollar to maintain its weakening bias
commodities_overall_why: PGIM says its positive view on commodities persists into 2026, supported by inflation and fiscally driven demand.
commodities_overall_raw_wording: Our positive view on commodities persists in 2026

currencies_jpy_why: Berenberg expects the yen to recover moderately as the Bank of Japan continues tightening.
currencies_jpy_raw_wording: This time, however, the JPY is likely to be helped mainly by the BoJ continuing its monetary tightening.
themes_geopolitical_risk_why: Geopolitical risks are explicitly expected to remain elevated and drive volatility spikes in 2026.
themes_geopolitical_risk_raw_wording: (geo)political risks are likely to remain elevated and volatility spikes are likely to recur.

source_file: Santander AM -Market Outlook 2026 Climbing towards new highs.pdf
equities_geography_japan_why: Santander sees Japanese equities supported by nominal growth, resilient earnings, and better corporate governance.
equities_geography_japan_raw_wording: Japan approaches 2026 with a combination of sustainable inflation, resilient corporate earnings, and structural reforms that underpin a constructive view on risk assets.
equities_geography_china_why: Santander views China as a tactical opportunity supported by policy easing and targeted fiscal support.
equities_geography_china_raw_wording: China may offer tactical opportunities supported by policy shifts.
fixed_income_duration_why: Santander favors an income-oriented bond stance with moderate rather than aggressive duration exposure.
fixed_income_duration_raw_wording: investors should favor an income-oriented strategy built on coupon accumulation, moderate duration, and limited credit exposure
currencies_usd_why: Santander expects the dollar to stay broadly range-bound in 2026 rather than trend sharply in either direction.
currencies_usd_raw_wording: The USD is expected to remain broadly stable in 2026.
themes_geopolitical_risk_why: Geopolitics is framed primarily as a live downside risk that can reprice regions and asset classes.
themes_geopolitical_risk_raw_wording: Trade, technology, and capital are being redirected by industrial policy, tariffs, and sanctions, fragmenting global supply chains.

global_growth_2026_why: M&G expects a resilient but only moderate global growth environment in 2026.
global_growth_2026_raw_wording: The consensus among economists is for a year of moderate growth and inflation in 2026.
us_gdp_2026_why: M&G expects U.S. growth to stay relatively solid with support from lower rates and tax cuts.
us_gdp_2026_raw_wording: US economic growth is relatively solid, and the combination of lower interest rates and tax cuts could provide tailwinds in the year ahead.
em_growth_2026_why: M&G expects emerging markets to remain structurally resilient with stronger growth and lower debt burdens than developed markets.
em_growth_2026_raw_wording: Emerging markets robust economic growth supported by a lower debt burden relative to developed markets.
us_inflation_2026_why: M&G expects U.S. inflation to remain above target and closer to 3% than 2%.
us_inflation_2026_raw_wording: inflation will be closer to 3% than 2%.
fixed_income_overall_why: M&G sees bonds as offering good value again, with attractive real yields and active opportunities across global fixed income.
fixed_income_overall_raw_wording: Given the attractive real yields on offer across fixed income markets now globally, bonds offer good value compared to equities, in our view.
fixed_income_segment_em_debt_why: M&G explicitly highlights EM debt as an attractive diversifier away from U.S. assets.
fixed_income_segment_em_debt_raw_wording: the emerging market debt universe appears an increasingly attractive alternative to US assets.

us_gdp_2026_why: BlackRock expects AI spending to keep U.S. growth resilient in 2026 even as the labor market cools.
us_gdp_2026_raw_wording: We see AI capital spending still supporting growth in 2026, allowing growth to hold up even as the labor market keeps cooling.
us_inflation_2026_why: BlackRock expects U.S. inflation to remain sticky above target.
us_inflation_2026_raw_wording: Yet inflation could prove sticky above the Fed's 2% target.
fixed_income_credit_quality_investment_g: neutral
BlackRock sees investment-grade carry as acceptable but not compelling enough for an overweight, especially at longer maturities.
Short-term IG credit: We are neutral... Long-term IG credit: We are underweight.
fixed_income_credit_quality_high_yield_w: BlackRock sees high yield carry as attractive, but expects greater issuer dispersion and stays neutral overall.
fixed_income_credit_quality_high_yield_r: Global high yield: We are neutral. High yield offers more attractive carry ... but we think dispersion between higher and weaker issuers will increase.
fixed_income_segment_government_bonds_wh: BlackRock is broadly cautious on long-duration developed-market government bonds, especially long U.S. Treasuries and Japanese government bonds.
fixed_income_segment_government_bonds_ra: Long U.S. Treasuries: We are underweight ... Japanese govt bonds: We are underweight.
currencies_em_fx_why: BlackRock acknowledges EM local-currency support from a weaker dollar but does not take a full positive stance because that dollar weakness may not persist.
currencies_em_fx_raw_wording: EM local currency: We are neutral. A weaker U.S. dollar has boosted local currency EM debt, but it's unclear if this weakening will persist.

us_gdp_2026_why: Candriam expects U.S. growth to slow after being supported by AI-linked investment.
eurozone_gdp_2026_why: Candriam expects a more supportive European policy mix in 2026 thanks to rearmament, German fiscal stimulus, and easier monetary policy.
eurozone_gdp_2026_raw_wording: Le plan ReArm Europe, la relance budgetaire allemande et l'assouplissement monetaire creeront un policy mix encore plus favorable en 2026.
us_inflation_2026_why: Candriam expects U.S. inflation to remain persistent even as the labor market slows.
us_inflation_2026_raw_wording: la Reserve federale se trouve prise en etau entre une inflation persistante et un marche du travail en deceleration.
fixed_income_segment_government_bonds_wh: Candriam argues government bonds have lost part of their traditional safe-haven role because stock-bond correlations have turned positive.
fixed_income_segment_government_bonds_ra: Les correlations positives entre actions et obligations reduisent le role traditionnel des emprunts d'Etat comme valeur refuge.
themes_geopolitical_risk_why: Candriam treats geopolitical tension and fragmentation as a persistent 2026 risk backdrop.
themes_geopolitical_risk_raw_wording: En 2026, les marches devront encore arbitrer entre promesses d'innovation et tensions geopolitiques.

equities_geography_europe_why: EFG expects renewed interest in European equities as German stimulus, defense spending, green investment, and lower rates improve the backdrop.
equities_geography_europe_raw_wording: We believe a renewed excitement for European and Swiss equities is likely.
themes_healthcare_why: EFG explicitly favors innovative healthcare exposure, especially smaller biotech-led drug development opportunities.
themes_healthcare_raw_wording: We favour exposure to smaller, innovative companies that are in a position to lead new drug development rather than larger, established healthcare companies.
themes_defense_sovereignty_why: EFG sees higher European defense spending and strategic restructuring as a durable investment support for 2026.
themes_defense_sovereignty_raw_wording: The EU will take further restructuring and reform steps in 2026 ... pressure from the US to increase defence spending.

global_growth_2026_why: DWS expects a solid but not classical global upswing in 2026, with growth still sufficient to support capital markets.
global_growth_2026_raw_wording: We do not expect a classical global upswing in 2026 and 2027 but we believe economic growth should provide a sufficiently solid foundation.
eur_usd_2026_why: DWS expects a structurally softer U.S. dollar over time rather than giving a clean point target.
eur_usd_2026_raw_wording: In our view, the U.S. dollar has partially lost its status as a safe haven and is likely to weaken over time.
fixed_income_credit_quality_investment_g: positive
DWS is constructive on corporate bonds overall and only asks for selectivity in high yield, implying a stronger preference for higher-quality credit.
Corporate bonds remain attractive in our view, despite very low credit spreads, thanks to the supportive macro environment and high demand.

currencies_em_fx_why: Goldman expects EM local-currency debt to benefit from Fed easing without major currency weakening, which is supportive for EM FX.
currencies_em_fx_raw_wording: Les obligations en devises locales ... des pays emergents devraient egalement en beneficier ... sans affaiblir sensiblement leur devise.

global_growth_2026_why: JP Morgan expects global growth to broaden across regions in 2026 under supportive monetary and fiscal policy.
global_growth_2026_raw_wording: Scenario de base : la croissance mondiale se generalise.
us_gdp_2026_why: JP Morgan expects U.S. growth to continue on rate cuts, tax support, and ongoing AI investment.
us_gdp_2026_raw_wording: La croissance americaine se poursuit soutenue par un nouvel assouplissement de la Reserve federale et les investissements continus dans l IA.
eurozone_gdp_2026_why: JP Morgan expects European growth to accelerate on significant fiscal stimulus and prior ECB easing.
eurozone_gdp_2026_raw_wording: La croissance europeenne devrait s accelerer grace a une relance budgetaire considerable et au soutien continu des precedentes baisses de taux.

eurozone_gdp_2026_why: BNP expects eurozone growth to regain momentum in 2026, helped by fading uncertainty and German fiscal support.
eurozone_gdp_2026_raw_wording: The Eurozone economy is likely to accelerate in 2026.
eurozone_inflation_2026_why: BNP expects further eurozone disinflation, with inflation undershooting the official target later in the horizon.
eurozone_inflation_2026_raw_wording: We see scope for further disinflation over the next couple of years.
fed_year_end_rate_2026_why: BNP expects meaningful Fed easing in 2026, taking U.S. policy rates below 3%.
fed_year_end_rate_2026_raw_wording: For 2026 this suggests substantial reductions in US interest rates to below 3%.
ecb_deposit_rate_year_end_2026_why: BNP expects the ECB to keep easing through 2026 if inflation undershoots target.
ecb_deposit_rate_year_end_2026_raw_wordi: We expect inflation to fall below target in 2027, which will prompt the European Central Bank to deliver a couple more interest rate cuts by the end of 2026.
equities_overall_why: BNP's summary view stays constructive on equities, with Europe and selected emerging markets highlighted as the most compelling opportunities.
equities_overall_raw_wording: European equities offer compelling value... In emerging markets, nations with strong technology sectors stand to benefit from lower US yields and a weaker dollar.
fixed_income_credit_quality_investment_g: positive
BNP is constructive on higher-quality credit thanks to attractive yields and healthy issuer fundamentals.
European investment grade bonds boasting yields of circa 3% to 3.5% ... European issuers' credit quality remains in good health.
themes_geopolitical_risk_why: Trade friction and geopolitical fragmentation remain explicit risks in BNP's 2026 outlook.
themes_geopolitical_risk_raw_wording: The global economy enters 2026 with surprising resilience, buoyed by a rebound in activity, despite ongoing trade frictions.

us_gdp_2026_why: AllianzGI expects the U.S. economy to stay resilient but slow to below-potential growth in 2026.
us_gdp_2026_raw_wording: The US economy is expected to remain resilient, though growth will slow to around 1.5-2%, slightly below potential.
eurozone_gdp_2026_why: AllianzGI expects a moderate cyclical eurozone recovery supported by incomes, easier policy, and German fiscal spending.
eurozone_gdp_2026_raw_wording: Europe is poised for a moderate cyclical recovery, with GDP growth expected at 1-1.5% in 2026.
us_inflation_2026_why: AllianzGI expects U.S. inflation to remain above target in 2026 because of tariff effects.
us_inflation_2026_raw_wording: US inflation is set to rise above 3%.
eurozone_inflation_2026_why: AllianzGI expects eurozone inflation to stay subdued and below target.
eurozone_inflation_2026_raw_wording: Inflation is projected to remain below 2%.
fed_year_end_rate_2026_why: AllianzGI expects the Fed to keep cutting in 2026 into the low-to-mid 3% range.
fed_year_end_rate_2026_raw_wording: The US Federal Reserve is expected to continue cutting rates in 2026, bringing the federal funds target range to 3.25-3.50%.
fixed_income_duration_why: AllianzGI sees developed-market duration as a useful source of resilience in a benign fixed-income backdrop.
fixed_income_duration_raw_wording: Developed markets duration offers resilience, while emerging market debt can provide yield enhancement and diversification.
fixed_income_credit_quality_investment_g: positive
AllianzGI explicitly favors investment-grade credit and crossover bonds over lower-quality spread product.
We continue to like investment grade credits and crossovers.
fixed_income_credit_quality_high_yield_w: AllianzGI sees high yield as expensive and less compelling than higher-quality credit.
fixed_income_credit_quality_high_yield_r: High yield spreads are expensively priced in our view.
currencies_eur_why: AllianzGI expects the euro to strengthen relative to peers as Europe faces less political pressure on monetary policy.
currencies_eur_raw_wording: The ECB and BoE are major central banks not under the yoke of heavy political pressure, strengthening the euro and sterling relative to their peers.

global_growth_2026_why: RBC expects moderate growth acceleration in 2026.
global_growth_2026_raw_wording: We forecast an environment of moderate economic growth.
us_gdp_2026_why: RBC forecasts 2026 US GDP growth at 2.2%.
eurozone_gdp_2026_why: RBC forecasts 2026 eurozone GDP growth at 1.3%.
china_gdp_2026_why: RBC forecasts 2026 China GDP growth at 4.4%.
em_growth_2026_why: RBC forecasts 2026 emerging-market GDP growth at 4.2%.
global_inflation_2026_why: Inflation should improve in 2026, but less than consensus expects.
global_inflation_2026_raw_wording: Inflation may not fall by quite as much as consensus expectations.
eurozone_inflation_2026_why: RBC forecasts 2026 eurozone CPI inflation at 2.0%.
fed_year_end_rate_2026_why: RBC forecasts the Fed funds upper bound at 3.25% by November 2026.
ecb_deposit_rate_year_end_2026_why: RBC forecasts the eurozone deposit facility rate at 2.00% in November 2026.
eur_usd_2026_why: RBC targets EUR/USD at 1.24 in November 2026.
equities_overall_why: RBC still expects equities to outperform bonds.
equities_overall_raw_wording: We expect stocks will continue to outperform bonds.
equities_geography_us_why: RBC increased US exposure but remains underweight because valuations are high.
equities_geography_us_raw_wording: we remain under weight U.S. equities given their relatively high valuations.
equities_geography_europe_why: RBC says Europe still trades at attractive valuations.
equities_geography_europe_raw_wording: Stock indices in Europe ... continue to trade at attractive distances
equities_geography_japan_why: RBC expects Japanese growth to stay positive with support from wages, fiscal stimulus, and corporate-governance reforms.
equities_geography_japan_raw_wording: Japan's economy will likely expand 1% for a second straight year in 2026. Wage gains and a fresh fiscal boost will offset softer exports caused by higher U.S. tariffs.
equities_geography_em_why: RBC sees emerging markets as attractively valued and forecasts double-digit total return.
equities_geography_em_raw_wording: emerging markets continue to trade at attractive distances
fixed_income_overall_why: Sovereign bonds are expected to deliver cash-like returns and portfolio ballast.
fixed_income_overall_raw_wording: sovereign bonds to deliver cash-like returns and offer ballast against equity-market volatility
fixed_income_segment_government_bonds_wh: RBC expects sovereign bonds to deliver limited but still useful defensive returns.
fixed_income_segment_government_bonds_ra: we expect sovereign bonds to deliver cash-like returns and offer ballast against equity-market volatility
currencies_usd_why: RBC expects the greenback to weaken again in 2026.
currencies_usd_raw_wording: resumption in the greenback's weakness sometime in 2026
currencies_eur_why: RBC targets EUR/USD higher at 1.24, implying euro strength versus the dollar.
currencies_jpy_why: RBC targets USD/JPY lower at 135, implying yen appreciation.
currencies_em_fx_why: EM currencies should benefit most from further USD depreciation.
currencies_em_fx_raw_wording: Emerging-market currencies ... will continue to be the main beneficiaries

themes_ai_raw_wording: the early stages of an AI-driven productivity boost
themes_geopolitical_risk_why: RBC flags global trade tensions as a key regional risk for 2026.
themes_geopolitical_risk_raw_wording: Key risks for the region in 2026 include global trade tensions

global_growth_2026_why: L&G expects global growth to stay resilient and roughly around trend, with the US as the swing factor.
global_growth_2026_raw_wording: global growth around trend ... the US economy as the swing factor
eurozone_gdp_2026_why: L&G expects euro area growth to strengthen gradually on manufacturing, rate cuts, and German spending.
eurozone_gdp_2026_raw_wording: The euro area has been more sluggish, but growth is expected to strengthen gradually
china_gdp_2026_why: China is expected to grow in the 4-5% range with policy support.
china_gdp_2026_raw_wording: China is expected to grow in the 4-5% range with government support

global_inflation_2026_raw_wording: inflation potentially on a path towards target

us_gdp_2026_raw_wording: The world's largest economy will likely determine which of a wide range of scenarios for global growth plays out in 2026.
em_growth_2026_why: L&G expects resilient growth across emerging markets and sees the risk skewed to the upside.
em_growth_2026_raw_wording: With resilient growth across emerging markets ... we believe the risk of a sudden reversal in capital flows is low.

fed_year_end_rate_2026_raw_wording: market consensus is that the US Federal Reserve is expected to ease by 100 basis points over the next 12 months

eur_usd_2026_raw_wording: we believe the US dollar is more likely to fall than rise given excessive valuations
equities_geography_europe_why: L&G sees opportunities in Europe as part of broader geographic diversification away from crowded US tech.
equities_geography_europe_raw_wording: complementing US exposures with allocations to Europe
equities_geography_em_why: The outlook explicitly flags an optimistic take on emerging markets.
equities_geography_em_raw_wording: offer an optimistic take on emerging markets
fixed_income_overall_why: L&G remains constructive on fixed income as a resilience tool, especially through Treasuries and a broader toolkit.
fixed_income_overall_raw_wording: we still see a place for US Treasuries
fixed_income_credit_quality_investment_g: positive
L&G explicitly adds investment-grade private credit for yield potential and diversification.
Investment grade private credit to gain yield potential and diversification

fixed_income_duration_raw_wording: short-dated Treasuries can serve as an effective hedge
fixed_income_segment_government_bonds_wh: L&G still sees a place for US Treasuries and other sovereigns in resilient portfolios.
fixed_income_segment_government_bonds_ra: we still see a place for US Treasuries
fixed_income_segment_inflation_linked_wh: L&G highlights stable, inflation-linked returns as attractive in the current environment.
currencies_usd_why: L&G believes the dollar is more likely to fall than rise because of excessive valuations.
currencies_usd_raw_wording: we believe the US dollar is more likely to fall than rise given excessive valuations
commodities_overall_why: L&G treats broad commodity exposure as a useful resilience tool.
commodities_overall_raw_wording: broad commodity ETFs can offer liquidity and simplicity
themes_ai_why: AI is framed as transformative, but over-investment and disruption risks are explicit.
themes_ai_raw_wording: AI may have a far bigger impact ... There is a danger of over-investment
themes_geopolitical_risk_why: Geopolitics is treated as a persistent source of market risk.
themes_geopolitical_risk_raw_wording: both geopolitics and domestic politics retain their ability to wrong-foot markets
themes_defense_sovereignty_why: Defense and infrastructure spending in Europe are part of the growth support story.
themes_defense_sovereignty_raw_wording: government spending in defence and infrastructure gaining traction

global_growth_2026_why: Credit Mutuel expects growth momentum to remain positive in 2026.
us_gdp_2026_why: US growth is supported by fiscal stimulus and still-resilient activity.
us_gdp_2026_raw_wording: Fiscal policies remain highly expansionary ... positive effects in 2026

eurozone_gdp_2026_raw_wording: Growth will remain limited but above potential growth, thanks in particular to investment plans in Germany.

us_inflation_2026_raw_wording: Inflation should stabilize slightly above 3% in 2026.

eurozone_inflation_2026_raw_wording: Inflation is expected to be slightly below the 2% target in 2026.

fed_year_end_rate_2026_raw_wording: The Fed will be constrained in future cuts due to activity rebound and inflationary pressures.

ecb_deposit_rate_year_end_2026_raw_wordi: The ECB is therefore likely to maintain the status quo in 2026
fixed_income_overall_why: The whole document is constructive on money markets, IG, and HY carry.
fixed_income_overall_raw_wording: Overall, we remain positive on money market funds
fixed_income_credit_quality_investment_g: positive
IG credit is supported by attractive carry and strong technicals.
High demand in the European IG credit market ... should provide technical support

fixed_income_credit_quality_high_yield_r: carry is expected to remain the primary driver of High Yield performance in 2026

fixed_income_duration_raw_wording: We maintain a positive duration stance ... favoring the belly of the curve (3-7 years).
fixed_income_segment_government_bonds_wh: They are overweight Germany and constructive on Italy and Spain in sovereigns.
fixed_income_segment_government_bonds_ra: We are overweight Germany, positive on Italy and Spain, and underweight France
fixed_income_segment_corporate_bonds_why: IG corporate spreads are still supported by demand and carry.
fixed_income_segment_corporate_bonds_raw: Credit spreads on investment grade bonds continued to hit record lows
themes_ai_why: AI demand supports parts of the macro backdrop, but AI bubble risk is explicitly flagged.
themes_ai_raw_wording: demand driven by the AI theme is strong ... Concerns about the AI bubble

us_gdp_2026_why: Lazard expects US growth to downshift materially because of trade and immigration policy.
us_gdp_2026_raw_wording: US trade policies and much stricter enforcement of immigration policies have led to a downshift in sustainable GDP growth from 1.7%-2.0% previously to perhaps 1.2%-1.5% looking forward.
eurozone_gdp_2026_why: German fiscal stimulus is expected to lift eurozone growth above consensus in 2026.
eurozone_gdp_2026_raw_wording: I expect this fiscal stimulus to meaningfully lift German GDP growth and to boost Eurozone GDP growth as well.
us_inflation_2026_why: Lazard expects tariffs to push US inflation above 3.5% in early 2026 rather than settle at target.
us_inflation_2026_raw_wording: US Consumer Price Index inflation rate is likely to top 3.5% in the first half of 2026.
eurozone_inflation_2026_why: Eurozone inflation is expected to fall below the ECB's 2% target.
eurozone_inflation_2026_raw_wording: Moreover, I expect inflation to fall below the European Central Bank's (ECB) 2% target.
equities_geography_japan_why: Lazard explicitly says Japanese equities look attractive despite only modest GDP growth.
equities_geography_japan_raw_wording: Japanese equities also look attractive, with policy support for consumption and corporate returns, even if GDP growth remains modest.
currencies_em_fx_why: Local-currency EM debt is attractive partly because of FX appreciation potential.
currencies_em_fx_raw_wording: local currency EM debt stands out for appreciation potential ... FX appreciation potential.
themes_geopolitical_risk_why: Geopolitical intervention is an explicit risk across Lazard's EM and Japan discussion.
themes_geopolitical_risk_raw_wording: Risks from geopolitical intervention remain, but similar risks are rising in the United States.

keeping the quality bias that has served us well late in the cycle

equities_geography_europe_raw_wording: Focus on ... opportunities in Europe and Asia
equities_geography_em_why: LGT wants more EM equities and bonds in the strategic allocation.
equities_geography_em_raw_wording: Add to holdings of emerging markets equities and bonds
equities_geography_china_why: China is highlighted as a market where AI adoption may already be ahead of the US.
equities_geography_china_raw_wording: China, for instance, is pushing hard for fast adoption
equities_size_small_cap_why: LGT explicitly favors small- and mid-sized private equity opportunities.
equities_size_small_cap_raw_wording: Focus on small- and mid-sized private equity
fixed_income_segment_em_debt_why: LGT wants more EM debt, especially local-currency exposure.
fixed_income_segment_em_debt_raw_wording: Shift from hard currency to local currency in EM debt
fixed_income_segment_inflation_linked_wh: Inflation-linked bonds are a named portfolio measure.
fixed_income_segment_inflation_linked_ra: Add to holdings of inflation-linked bonds
currencies_usd_why: LGT hedges most USD exposure back to base currency.
currencies_usd_raw_wording: Hedge most US dollar exposure back to base currency
currencies_em_fx_why: LGT leaves EM and frontier FX unhedged to harvest carry.
currencies_em_fx_raw_wording: Leave emerging and frontier market FX unhedged to capitalize on the positive interest rate carry
commodities_gold_why: Gold is one of the explicit resilience tools in the LGT allocation playbook.
fixed_income_overall_why: LGT is reallocating fixed income away from high yield toward IG bonds, EM debt, and inflation protection rather than making a one-way duration call.
fixed_income_overall_raw_wording: shift from high-yield bonds to ... inflation-linked bonds
fixed_income_credit_quality_investment_g: positive
LGT's strategic allocation favors investment-grade bonds over high yield.
Investment-grade bonds 9.0% ... High-yield bonds 1.0%
equities_overall_why: LGT is increasing public equities outside the US within its strategic allocation.
equities_overall_raw_wording: a shift from high-yield bonds to public equities outside the US
equities_style_quality_why: LGT says its public-market mandates maintain a strong focus on sustainability and quality.
equities_style_quality_raw_wording: we maintain a strong focus on sustainability and quality
themes_ai_why: LGT remains constructive on AI buildout, but explicitly warns about over-investment and labor disruption.
themes_ai_raw_wording: baseline view is that the buildout push is continuing for now, but ... two ways that this could go wrong
themes_geopolitical_risk_why: Geopolitical crises and unstable multipolarity are central risks.
themes_defense_sovereignty_why: National security, industrial policy, and sovereignty concerns are explicit drivers in the outlook.
themes_defense_sovereignty_raw_wording: Alignment with the government's evolving policy priorities has now become a critical success factor

global_growth_2026_why: Royal London sees the cycle still in recovery, but with markets moving fast and valuations richer.
global_growth_2026_raw_wording: Investment Clock is in Recovery, but moving fast
global_inflation_2026_why: The last mile of inflation remains a two-sided risk for 2026.
global_inflation_2026_raw_wording: There is inflation's 'last mile' to watch.
equities_overall_why: Royal London remains invested but stresses diversification over concentration in expensive markets.
equities_geography_us_why: They remain tactically positive on US equities and technology, but have reduced strategic US exposure.
equities_geography_us_raw_wording: we remain tactically positive on US equities and technology ... we've further reduced our strategic exposure to US equities
fixed_income_overall_why: Royal London sees bonds as useful core protection in a volatile environment.
fixed_income_overall_raw_wording: the right mix is a globally diversified government bond core
fixed_income_segment_government_bonds_wh: Government bonds offer value and are part of the recommended core mix.
fixed_income_credit_quality_investment_g: positive
Royal London says investment grade credit still offers attractive yields backed by robust fundamentals.
investment grade credit still offers attractive yields underpinned by robust credit fundamentals
fixed_income_segment_corporate_bonds_why: Royal London remains constructive on sterling credit for income generation, while stressing selectivity.
fixed_income_segment_corporate_bonds_raw: credit continues to compensate investors for fundamental risks and provides potential for attractive income generation
fixed_income_segment_inflation_linked_wh: Inflation-linked debt is recommended as relatively cheap protection.
fixed_income_segment_inflation_linked_ra: Inflation-linked debt can give investors protection
fed_year_end_rate_2026_why: Royal London expects lower inflation and more rate cuts in 2026, but with a jagged path for the Fed.
fed_year_end_rate_2026_raw_wording: Lower inflation and more rate cuts are likely in 2026, but the path won't be smooth.
ecb_deposit_rate_year_end_2026_why: Royal London notes the ECB held steady after earlier cuts, with inflation still a key debate into 2026.
ecb_deposit_rate_year_end_2026_raw_wordi: ECB held steady in the fourth quarter after earlier cuts
commodities_overall_why: Royal London continues to allocate to commodities as a hedge against unexpected inflation and geopolitical risk.
commodities_overall_raw_wording: we continue to allocate to commodities, including gold, as a hedge
commodities_gold_why: Gold is one of the assets Royal London highlights as having continued to rise.
commodities_gold_raw_wording: gold has continued to drive ever higher
commodities_industrial_metals_why: Industrial metals are positioned in the recovery section of the investment clock.
themes_ai_why: Royal London sees AI as a game changer, but also as a source of valuation and monetization risk.
themes_ai_raw_wording: Artificial intelligence is undoubtedly a game changer ... we are already seeing that there will be winners and losers
themes_energy_transition_why: Royal London frames the energy transition as a resilience theme, especially around grids, storage, and renewables.
themes_energy_transition_raw_wording: The energy transition is becoming a resilience play
themes_geopolitical_risk_why: Geopolitical volatility is an explicit source of ongoing market risk.
